danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy character sprites representing revived antagonists
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring player intervention; markers of hostile world state

danger_signals.visual_form: Vessel/craft visual in cutscenes and thematic staging
danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonistic force; source of danger and world corruption

danger_signals.visual_form: Environmental theming tied to each Dastardly Bomber's influence
danger_signals.meaning: Corruption of natural world; visual representation of havoc caused by enemies

safety_signals.signal: White Bomber and Black Bomber presence
safety_signals.meaning: Player agency and heroic force capable of restoring order

safety_signals.visual_form: Linear stage advancement and narrative cutscenes
safety_signals.meaning: Clear path to victory and world restoration; structured safety framework

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Individual character designs representing each member

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Each controls or corrupts an element-themed star region

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: UFO vessel; creator/controller of Dastardly Bombers
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: UFO junkyard origin; oversees revival of bombers; final stage threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Each character represents a country of Earth; includes Pretty Bomber as France

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Element-themed stars under control of Five Dastardly Bombers; havoc indicated through environmental degradation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies actively causing disruption; Bagular working to maintain control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: White Bomber and Black Bomber adventuring across corrupted stages; cutscenes showing narrative advancement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must traverse each element-themed star to defeat bombers and restore order

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Defeat of individual Dastardly Bombers; progression to next corrupted star
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Advance through story; approach final confrontation with Bagular

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bagular encountered in UFO; final battle scenario
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ultimate antagonist defeated; world threat eliminated

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of the Five Dastardly Bombers' stages has distinct elemental theming, visually communicating their domain of influence and the type of havoc they cause
Bagular's introduction in a junkyard establishes technological threat; UFO visual grounds the antagonistic force in a specific origin point
Visual depiction of Dastardly Bombers being revived on tables within UFO communicates the artificial nature of the threat and Bagular's scientific/technological power
Story mode cutscenes communicate plot progression, antagonist motivations, and world state through visual narrative rather than gameplay alone

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Causing havoc on assigned element-themed stars; creating corrupted world state requiring player intervention
Directing operations from UFO; orchestrating revival and maintenance of threats; final boss encounter
Adventuring across corrupted stars; actively combating threats; narrative protagonist
Accompanying White Bomber; co-protagonist in restoration effort

notes: Super Bomberman 3 employs a straightforward good vs. evil visual communication structure. The game does not feature complex political faction dynamics; instead, it presents a binary hero-villain paradigm with clear visual distinction between protagonists (Bombers) and antagonists (Dastardly Bombers, Bagular). The international character representation in multiplayer Battle Mode serves cosmetic/identity purposes rather than faction-based conflict signaling. Story mode communicates world state primarily through narrative progression and elemental stage theming. The game's simplified graphics compared to predecessors were a deliberate stylistic choice, making visual communication of threats and safety more direct and less nuanced. Cutscene-driven storytelling contrasts with the arcade-style gameplay, creating two distinct channels for world-state communication.
